vulnerability.title: Build and Test AIDE Database
vulnerability.description: Run the following command to generate a new database: $ sudo /usr/sbin/aide --init By default, the database will be written to the file /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz. Storing the database, the configuration file /etc/aide.conf, and the binary /usr/sbin/aide (or hashes of these files), in a secure location (such as on read-only media) provides additional assurance about their integrity. The newly-generated database can be installed as follows: $ sudo cp /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz To initiate a manual check, run the following command: $ sudo /usr/sbin/aide --check If this check produces any unexpected output, investigate.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then if ! rpm -q --quiet "aide" ; then dnf install -y "aide" fi /usr/sbin/aide --init /bin/cp -p /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only installs the aide package (if missing), initializes the AIDE database, and copies a new database file. These actions are standard, low‑impact operations that do not affect authentication, networking, or boot configuration and are unlikely to cause lockout or service disruption.
